sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), value FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value, created_at) VALUES (1, 'UpperCase', 10.5, NOW()), (2, NULL, 15.0, NOW()), (3, '', 5.5, NOW()), (4, 'lowercase', 0.0, NOW());
SELECT id, lcase(name) AS lower_name, value FROM test_table WHERE lcase(name) <= 'lowercase' AND value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, AVG(value) AS avg_value FROM test_table WHERE lcase(name) <= 'uppercase' GROUP BY id HAVING avg_value <= 10.0;

SELECT DISTINCT t1.id FROM (SELECT id, lcase(name) AS lower_name FROM test_table WHERE value > (SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE id = 3)) AS t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON lcase(t1.lower_name) <= lcase(t2.name) WHERE t2.created_at IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE lcase(name) <= lcase(NULLIF((SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE value = 5.5), '')) AND created_at < NOW() ORDER BY id DESC;
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INT, ref_name VARCHAR(50), FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO another_table (ref_id, ref_name) VALUES (1, 'Reference'), (2, NULL), (3, 'value check');
SELECT a.ref_id, lcase(a.ref_name) FROM another_table a JOIN test_table t ON a.ref_id <= t.id WHERE lcase(a.ref_name) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COALESCE(lcase(name), 'Unknown') FROM test_table WHERE value <= 10.0;
